title: Deployment contract (Contract 5)
description: Deployment descriptor published per component release (infergate publishes one per release; inferops consumes it; inference-lab pins it). Normative. It declares HOW a component is deployed and operated: pinned image, ports, environment and config mounts, probe semantics (including warm-up-aware readiness: readiness MUST be false during model load/warm-up), model mount, resources including GPU count, graceful termination (preStop drain hook; termination grace period strictly greater than the max stream duration), and secret EXPECTATIONS (names and purposes only — never values). It is a contract about operational behavior, not a Kubernetes manifest; inferops derives manifests from it.

component.description: Component this descriptor deploys (e.g. infergate, vllm-backend, mock-backend).

release.description: Component release version this descriptor was published for (a descriptor is published per release).

image.description: Container image pin. The DIGEST is the pin; tags are informative only and MUST NOT be the deployment reference.

tag.description: Informative only; deployments resolve by digest.

digest.description: Content digest the deployment MUST run.

ports.description: Named ports. The API port serves Contract 1; the metrics port serves the Contract 2 vocabulary.

env.description: Environment variables the component expects. Each entry is either a literal value or a reference to a secret declared under 'secrets' (by name). Secret VALUES never appear in this descriptor.

from_secret.description: Name of an entry under 'secrets' (delivery=env) supplying this variable.

config_mounts.description: Configuration files/directories mounted into the container. May be empty when the component is configured by env only.

reload_semantics.description: How config changes take effect (e.g. atomic snapshot swap without dropping streams — fault scenario fs-08).

probes.description: Probe semantics. NORMATIVE: readiness is warm-up-aware — it MUST report false during model load/warm-up so no traffic arrives before the component is warm (fault scenario fs-11); the startup probe budget (period_seconds x failure_threshold) MUST cover worst-case model load so liveness never kills a loading replica (no restart loops).

warm_up.description: Warm-up-aware readiness (REQUIRED): the descriptor MUST state what 'warm' means for this component and attest that readiness is gated on it.

ready_only_after_warm_up.description: NORMATIVE attestation: readiness reports false until warm_signal holds. A descriptor without this gate is non-conformant.

warm_signal.description: Concrete condition that defines warm (e.g. model weights loaded and first inference completed; config snapshot loaded and backend probes completed).

expected_warm_up_seconds.description: Expected worst-case warm-up duration; the startup probe budget MUST cover it.

model_mount.description: Model artifact mount. REQUIRED for components that serve a model; null is permitted ONLY for components that serve no model (e.g. the gateway).

type.description: Volume kind expected (e.g. persistent-volume-claim, host-path, image-baked).

expected_content.description: What must be present at the mount (checkpoint id + revision), pinning the model identity for the benchmark comparability rule.

resources.description: Resource requests/limits including GPU count (0 for GPU-free components such as the gateway — basic development and CI never require a GPU).

type.description: GPU model expected (e.g. NVIDIA A10G); cross-reference a hardware-profile via hardware_profile_ref where one exists.

hardware_profile_ref.description: profile_id of the hardware-profile this deployment targets.

graceful_termination.description: Graceful termination semantics (fault scenarios fs-05/fs-12): a preStop drain hook stops acceptance and lets accepted streams complete; the termination grace period MUST be strictly greater than the max stream duration so no in-flight stream is force-killed.

initiates_drain.description: NORMATIVE: the preStop hook MUST initiate drain — readiness goes false, no new requests are accepted, in-flight streams run to completion.

action.description: What the hook does (e.g. call /admin drain endpoint; sleep for endpoint-removal propagation then signal drain).

max_stream_duration_seconds.description: The component-enforced upper bound on any single stream's duration.

grace_period_exceeds_max_stream_duration.description: NORMATIVE validation rule, encoded as a required attestation because JSON Schema cannot compare sibling numerics: termination_grace_period_seconds MUST be strictly greater than max_stream_duration_seconds (plus drain overhead). The publisher asserts it here; validators that can compare numbers SHOULD additionally enforce the arithmetic, and a descriptor where the arithmetic does not hold is non-conformant even if this field is present.

secrets.description: Secret EXPECTATIONS: what secrets must exist and why — names, purposes, delivery. Values, key material, and tokens MUST NOT appear anywhere in this descriptor (security policy). May be empty.

required.description: false = the component starts without it (degraded mode stated in purpose).

cpu.description: Kubernetes CPU quantity (cores or millicores, e.g. 2, 500m).
